summary: The first_proof_2 dataset comprises 10 open research-level mathematics problems spanning descriptive set theory, piecewise-linear geometry, probability, and Riemannian geometry, each currently carrying exactly one open proof issue and zero resolutions. Activity is uniformly distributed (one open issue per problem, 100 proof-status entries apiece), indicating an early, exploratory stage with no problem yet showing breakout traction. One housekeeping artifact (rm-01413, a VOID probe issue created in error) should be ignored and closed.
problems: All 10 problems (prob-01 through prob-10) sit at 1 open / 0 resolved — no problem has yet converged to a verified proof.
Every open issue follows the identical 'Proof: Problem N: <topic>' naming pattern, suggesting auto-generated stubs rather than substantive in-progress work.
Uniform 100-issue proof status across q1–q10 gives no differentiating signal on which problems are closest to solution.
rm-01413 is a stray VOID probe issue created in error and is polluting the issue list.
Zero resolved issues dataset-wide means no validated solution pipeline has completed end-to-end on this batch.
highlights: Clean, consistent issue structure: one well-formed open issue per problem makes the dataset easy to triage.
Broad mathematical coverage (descriptive set theory, PL geometry, probability, Riemannian geometry) provides diverse testing ground for the solver.
Substantial proof-status depth (100 entries per problem) supplies ample material to mine for promising partial arguments.
The erroneous probe issue is already flagged VOID, so cleanup is low-risk.

suggested_todos.title: Close/purge the VOID probe issue rm-01413 to clean the issue list
Triage the 100 proof-status entries per problem to surface the 2-3 problems with the strongest partial proofs and prioritize them for next week
Identify likely-soonest-solvable problems (combinatorial/identity-style, e.g. prob-09 Combinatorial Identities and prob-03 Weighted Bernoulli Sums) for focused solver runs
Add resolution/progress metadata to open issues so traction can be measured beyond raw open/resolved counts
Standardize issue titles to include difficulty/area tags to enable better cross-problem prioritization
